command_shape: run_microbench.py --device cuda --dtype bfloat16 --preset full --batch 1 --seqlen 512 --attention-impl sparse-gather-lightning-indexer --moe-impl grouped-expert-batches-dynamic-compact-reduce-bmm-out-dgrad-dense-wgrad --shared-expert-impl fused-w13 --profile-blocks --warmup 0 --iters 1

failure.message: HIP out of memory. Tried to allocate 126.00 GiB.

interpretation: Full BF16/reference dynamic MoE did not use the compact packed-sim path; it fell back to token-sum/index-select materialization and OOMed. The LightningIndexer path had already executed earlier in the forward path, so this is a MoE reference fallback limitation.
next_action: Use the fp4-packed-sim compact MoE path for full-shape integrated LightningIndexer probes, or implement a BF16 compact-native grouped MoE path that avoids materialized expert weight selection.
